meta.title: Receipt Invoice Generator - ChatGPT Think Mode Crosscheck Plan
meta.created_for: Cross-check by ChatGPT Think Mode before generating Claude audit output or scaffolding application code
meta.important_boundary: This JSON is a planning and audit handoff artifact only. It must not be treated as application source code, migration output, or production configuration.
meta.latest_update: Integrated ChatGPT deep research corrections, amount-in-words feature, and multi-page PDF pagination requirements.

purpose_for_chatgpt_think_mode.primary_goal: Audit whether the Claude audit-output plan improves the original Codex audit pack while preserving the user's workflow and locked decisions.
what_to_review: Compare the original audit pack plan against the proposed Claude audit output structure.
Check whether Codex's crosscheck adjustments are technically sound and aligned with the user's business intent.
Verify that Excel, PDF, and image template facts are preserved accurately enough for future implementation agents.
Identify any remaining gaps before application scaffolding begins.
Return a concise but rigorous improvement list, especially where schema, API, e-Invoice readiness, PDF rendering, Telegram, DeepSeek, and deployment choices could drift from scope.
expected_output_from_chatgpt: A verdict on whether the Claude audit-output plan should be accepted as adjusted.
Any additional corrections needed before writing the seven markdown audit files.
A list of risks or missing information not already captured here.
A confirmation that locked decisions remain unchanged.
A recommendation on whether this crosscheck JSON is sufficient for a downstream implementation agent.
do_not_do: Do not reopen locked decisions unless a critical contradiction is found.
Do not convert historical Excel data into production records.
Do not promote full MyInvois submission into v1.
Do not redesign company document templates from scratch.
Do not merge company numbering or template identities.

historical_excel_policy.source_exact: Template reference only, not imported as production records.
historical_excel_policy.user_intent: Excel lama reference only, bukan import production.
historical_excel_policy.implementation_guardrail: No old workbook row, document number, customer, item, or total becomes live production data unless the user later starts a separate import project.

document_design_policy.source_exact: Match plus kemas: preserve original design identity while improving spacing, typos, table consistency, and polish.
document_design_policy.implementation_guardrail: Templates should visually follow the supplied company formats, but cleanup spacing, typo-level text, wrapping, alignment, table consistency, and professional polish.

company_isolation.source_exact: Every company has separate profile, templates, records, numbering, and sequences.
company_isolation.implementation_guardrail: All database queries, renderers, API calls, Telegram flows, and sequence counters must be scoped by company_id.

numbering_issue_rule.source_exact: Official numbers are generated only when a document is issued, not while it is a draft.
numbering_issue_rule.implementation_guardrail: Draft documents use internal draft IDs only; final invoice/quotation/receipt/DO numbers are allocated transactionally at issue time.

einvoice_v1_scope.source_exact: Metadata-ready only. MyInvois submission/signing/cancel/reject is not part of v1.
einvoice_v1_scope.implementation_guardrail: Store future MyInvois fields and validation readiness, but do not build official submission, digital signing, cancel, reject, or reconciliation in v1.

no_cross_company_numbering.source_exact: Wehdah, Persada, Virtue Damsel, and Nas Ceria numbering must never share one sequence.
no_cross_company_numbering.user_intent: Jangan campur numbering Wehdah, Persada, Virtue Damsel, Nas Ceria.
no_cross_company_numbering.implementation_guardrail: Sequence scope must be company_id + document_type + year, with optional branch/project extension later.

workspace_state.source_exact: Current workspace is empty, so build a new system rather than refactor an existing app.
workspace_state.user_intent: Workspace asal kosong, bina sistem baru.
workspace_state.implementation_guardrail: Future implementation should scaffold a new application and treat handoff folders as planning/reference material.

current_repo_observation_before_this_fil.note: The Claude audit-output plan supplied by the user is being evaluated as proposed content, not as an existing diff.

original_plan_summary.application_goal: Build a premium multi-company receipt, invoice, quotation, delivery order, and related document generator to replace scattered Excel workflows.

target_server.deployment_intent: Single Laravel monolith with queues, PDF rendering, backups, SSL, and future API/bot integrations.

core_features: Company selector before creating any document.
Per-company profile, template, record, and numbering isolation.
Draft and issue lifecycle with official number allocated only on issue.
A4 PDF output with company-specific template identity.
60mm thermal receipt output for receipt/quick print.
Artwork attachment pages for Wehdah-style printing/design jobs.
Telegram bot flow for draft creation and preview.
DeepSeek as optional parser/drafter, never as source of truth.
e-Invoice metadata readiness for future MyInvois integration.
Audit trail, immutable issued PDFs, backups, and production smoke checks.
original_expected_claude_deliverables: A revised plan with clear improvements and no ambiguity for implementers.
A risk register with severity and mitigation.
A missing-info checklist grouped by company and workflow.
Recommended implementation phase ordering and acceptance gates.
Any proposed schema or API changes that reduce future rework.
original_audit_angles: Document scope sufficiency for Malaysia SME and global use.
Data model coverage for invoice, quotation, DO, receipt, payment, refund, credit note, debit note, purchase order, cash bill, and voucher workflows.
Collision-safe per-company and per-document numbering policy.
PDF renderer suitability for Excel-style templates.
e-Invoice metadata sufficiency for later MyInvois.
Tencent Ubuntu security, backups, monitoring, and maintainability.
Template audit separation between canonical company templates and mixed sample sheets.

claude_plan_summary.proposed_change_shape: Seven new markdown audit files under a sibling output folder; original input pack byte-identical; no app code, migrations, or infrastructure.

proposed_files.purpose: Lists the six audit deliverables and recommended read order.
Executive entry point with verdict, top risks, missing-info blockers, and recommended changes.
Main revised plan organized by the original audit angles.
Risk table with severity, likelihood, impact, mitigation, and owner.
Checklist grouped by company, system/deployment, and workflow-level decisions.
Re-baselined phases with explicit acceptance gates.
Concrete proposed schema and API changes to reduce future rework.

positive_structure_choices: Places output beside, not inside, the original pack.
Keeps original input pack untouched so reference manifest remains valid.
Covers all five expected Claude deliverables.
Uses `00-summary.md` as the human-readable executive entry point.
Writes risk register last because it depends on findings from every other deliverable.
Requires each markdown file to end with a source references section.
claimed_verification_from_claude_plan: Every risk row has mitigation and cross-reference.
Diff against main contains only the seven output files.
claude_plan_summary.codex_observation: At the time of this crosscheck JSON, the proposed Claude output folder is not yet present in the local repo. The plan should be implemented later as an audit-output pass.

codex_crosscheck_verdict.overall_verdict: The plan is acceptable for revised audit output and DeepSeek implementation handoff generation, but still blocked for actual app scaffolding until Phase 0.5 freezes canonical sources, numbering policies, official_receipt taxonomy, issue snapshots, and template identity.
codex_crosscheck_verdict.why_not_application_code: The requested pass is audit deliverables only. It should not scaffold Laravel, create migrations, configure deployment, or write production templates.
best_additions_from_claude: Executive summary file for fast review.
Formal risk register with severity, likelihood, impact, mitigation, and owner.
Grouped missing-info checklist with phase blockers.
Measurable phase gates and test names.
Schema/API improvement document for idempotency, status history, payment allocation, immutable PDF renders, line-level e-Invoice fields, and safer issue endpoint behavior.
needs_adjustment: Refund note and self-billed invoice should not become full v1 UI workflows unless the user later asks. They should be noted as future MyInvois metadata/readiness concepts.
Schema sketches in the audit output must be labelled Proposed, not final migrations.
Issued PDF immutability must remain strict. Template edits must create future versions, not mutate historical issued PDFs.
PDF content-hash dedup must be scoped by company_id, document_id, template_version, and render input hash to prevent cross-company leakage.
MyInvois crosscheck should rely on current official SDK docs when the audit output is written, then re-verify again before Phase 5.
INDEX link verification must run from the output folder or use full paths, otherwise relative path checks may be misleading.
The draft PR step is execution workflow, not part of the markdown deliverable content itself.
codex_crosscheck_verdict.decision: Accept Claude's structure after applying Codex adjustments. The adjusted plan remains aligned with the user's workflow: reference-only Excel, match-plus-kemas design, isolated company records and sequences, issue-time numbering, metadata-ready e-Invoice, and no app code in this audit pass.
new_user_feature_requests: Optional amount-in-words rendering for totals, with Malay MYR default and issue-time snapshot.
Multi-page PDF pagination so item-heavy invoice/quotation/DO outputs remain clean and reference-aligned.

accepted_claude_improvements.reason: Keeps the original audit pack byte-identical while allowing reviewed audit deliverables to sit beside it.
Maps cleanly to the expected Claude deliverables and makes review order explicit.
The user wants clarity and confidence; summary-first keeps the audit usable.
Adds severity, likelihood, impact, mitigation, and ownership for risks that were implicit in the original pack.
Turns open company/deployment questions into an actionable gate list.

accepted_claude_improvements.improvement: Phase 0.5 missing info and v1 scope freeze
accepted_claude_improvements.reason: Prevents scaffolding choices from being made before key company, numbering, and scope decisions are settled.
Protects against double issue, queue retry, API retry, and Telegram confirmation mismatch.
Classification, tax, and exemption details often belong at item level, so storing only document-level metadata would create future rework.
SSH key only, fail2ban, UFW default-deny, local-only PostgreSQL, restore drills, and SSL alerts are appropriate for a single Ubuntu production server.
Prevents mixed workbook sheets from leaking into company identity, template, or numbering seeds.
Separates formal official receipt lifecycle from thermal quick-print output.
Protects issued documents from later company/customer/bank/template profile edits.
Matches existing Wehdah-style RM amount-in-words sections and user request for optional text totals.
Prevents item-heavy documents from overlapping totals, signatures, artwork, or footers.

claude_items_to_adjust.adjustment: Mention as future MyInvois metadata/readiness and possible later document types, not as full v1 UI workflows.
claude_items_to_adjust.reason: Locked v1 scope already has ten document types and e-Invoice is metadata-ready only.

claude_items_to_adjust.adjustment: Mark all schema as Proposed, with rationale and risk mitigation, not final migration instructions.
claude_items_to_adjust.reason: No application scaffold exists yet; audit output should guide but not prematurely freeze implementation details.

claude_items_to_adjust.adjustment: If recommended, restrict to drafts, template versions, and explicit replacement/revision records. Issued rendered PDFs remain immutable.
claude_items_to_adjust.reason: The user needs trustworthy historical records and no silent mutation of issued documents.

claude_items_to_adjust.adjustment: Scope dedup by company_id, document_id, template_version, render_input_hash, and storage_path authorization.
claude_items_to_adjust.reason: Raw content hash dedup alone can create cross-company disclosure or wrong-document reuse risks.

claude_items_to_adjust.adjustment: Say verified against official MyInvois SDK at audit-writing time and must be re-verified before Phase 5.
claude_items_to_adjust.reason: The MyInvois schema and code lists can change; official docs are the correct source of truth.

claude_items_to_adjust.adjustment: Run link checks from inside `handoff/claude-audit-output-2026-05-17` or prefix paths with that folder.
claude_items_to_adjust.reason: The proposed shell loop using paths extracted from INDEX may fail from repo root if paths are relative.

claude_items_to_adjust.adjustment: Require branch based on latest origin/main and verify only seven new audit-output files before PR.
claude_items_to_adjust.reason: The repo may have local changes later; the audit pass must not accidentally include unrelated files.

design_policy.meaning: Follow each company's existing document identity while improving layout discipline, table consistency, readable spacing, long-text wrapping, professional typography, and minor wording/typo issues.
not_allowed: Blindly clone messy Excel data as production records.
Use one generic template for all companies.
Let Persada use Wehdah or A To Z branding by accident.
Let old document numbers drive production sequence.
Use a full redesign that loses the supplied company identity.

nas_ceria_q_3118.layout_pattern: Portrait quote/invoice with company details at top, customer block, prepared-by field, item table, subtotal/total, bank/account footer.
nas_ceria_q_3118.non_document_sheet: Modal contains costing/purchase list and receipt references; it is not a billing template.
design_improvements: Keep the familiar compact table but enforce consistent column widths and wrapping.
Move old Vertex42 copyright/helper text out of production output.
Keep bank details/footer but make editable per company profile.
Standardize date formatting instead of raw Excel serial behavior.
Ensure long masjid addresses wrap without overlapping totals or footer.
nas_ceria_q_3118.guardrail: All sheet numbers such as 3118, 2291, 2292, 2293, 2351, and 3121 are historical examples only.

design_improvements: Separate invoice/quotation templates from costing sheets in future UI.
Do not expose hidden legacy sheets as selectable templates.
Support long project descriptions and grouped item sections without breaking page layout.
Support discount rows and deposit/balance style invoice rows where used, but model them explicitly.
nas_ceria_masjid_muaz_project.guardrail: This workbook is a high-risk source for accidental data/template confusion; downstream agents must classify each sheet before using it.

wehdah_template_group.style_family: Lao UI/Rockwell invoice and quotation family
wehdah_template_group.font_pattern: Mostly Lao UI 13/14/16/20 with Rockwell or Rockwell Extra Bold title areas
wehdah_template_group.color_pattern: Dark blue header/title fills around FF002060, grey fills, medium outer borders, thin inner table borders
common_document_layout: Company identity and address at top
Document title block, usually INVOICE or QUOTATION
No, Date, Payment Term, Currency metadata block
Item table with Item, Description, Qty, Unit, Unit Price, Discount, Total Price
Amount in words with RM prefix
Yours faithfully and goods received condition text
Authorised Signature and Company Sign & Chop

future_system_behavior: Allow optional upload of artwork images/PDFs.
Append artwork page after main document.
Place artwork in clean table/grid with captions Artwork 1, Artwork 2, etc.
Add confirmation line and company sign/chop area.
Preserve originals and generate preview thumbnails.

design_improvements: Fix spelling/grammar in configurable terms without losing meaning.
Normalize table header alignment and currency formatting.
Prevent artwork pages from overflowing or cropping large images.
Use font availability checks inside Docker so Lao UI/Rockwell fallback does not destroy layout.
Support long item descriptions and multi-line design/poster lists.
Keep bank details editable per company and do not hard-code in template CSS.
wehdah_template_group.guardrail: Wehdah templates often include A To Z blank template sheets; those are structural references only and must not become Wehdah company data unless explicitly mapped.

inv_signage_pdf.page_1: Issued invoice page with Wehdah header, customer Muhammad Hakim / Masjid Al-Muttaqin, item table, grand total, bank details, terms, signature and company chop areas.
inv_signage_pdf.page_2: Artwork page with artwork labels and confirmation/sign-chop section.
inv_signage_pdf.design_importance: This PDF is the clearest output target for Wehdah invoice plus artwork attachment workflow.
future_visual_qa: Confirm page count when artwork is attached.
Confirm page 1 remains invoice-only and page 2 contains artwork grid.
Confirm artwork captions and confirmation text appear.
Confirm total and bank details are not pushed off page by long descriptions.

persada_assets_and_workbook.non_canonical_warning: Other workbook sheets contain A To Z Account, Virtue Damsel, and Wehdah data. Do not treat them as Persada billing truth.

assets.usage: Persada logo/wordmark; JPG with white background.
Transparent blue company stamp/chop near signature area.
A4 letterhead background for formal letters/proposals; may also guide invoice header/footer design.

design_improvements: Resolve address wording difference between workbook and letterhead before production.
Use Persada navy/green identity consistently.
Keep letterhead as a formal document background, not necessarily as invoice background unless user confirms.
Use stamp overlay only in intended sign/chop area.
Confirm whether website appears on invoice/receipt PDFs.

virtue_damsel_partial_reference.observed_in: Mixed sheet inside PERSADA GEMILANG GLOBAL.xlsx
observed_identity: No 30, RD 3, Taman Ramal Desa, 43000 Kajang Selangor
virtue_damsel_partial_reference.design_rule: Build data model capability for Virtue, but do not finalize logo/stamp/bank/template until user confirms active v1 scope and supplies missing assets.

a_to_z_generic_templates.guardrail: Do not seed A To Z Account as company profile unless user later confirms it as an actual company.

global_rules: User must choose company before choosing document type.
Document template selection must be filtered by company_id and document_type.
Renderer must assert that document.company_id equals template.company_id before rendering.
All bank, footer, signature, stamp, tax, contact, and logo values come from editable company profile/template settings.
No company can reuse another company's sequence counter.
Every issued document stores template_version and rendered PDF path.

wehdah_solution.special_required_control: Artwork attachment toggle and ordered artwork page grid.

persada_gemilang_global.canonical_template_source: PERSADA GEMILANG GLOBAL.xlsx sheet INV 1 plus Persada assets

nas_ceria_services.special_required_control: Long masjid project description wrapping and explicit separation of costing sheets from customer-facing documents.

virtue_damsel_solution.rule: Do not finalize template or seed complete company data from partial mixed sheet.

draft_to_issue: Draft may be edited freely and has only internal draft_id.
Preview PDF should include draft status or not allocate official number.
Issue allocates final number inside DB transaction.
Issue stores immutable rendered PDF version.
Issue writes audit log and document_status_history.
numbering: Use row-locked sequence counter per company_id + document_type + issue_year.
Use issue-time timezone consistently; avoid async queue year-boundary drift by determining issue_year in the same transaction as sequence allocation.
Use idempotency key for issue endpoint and Telegram confirmation.
Manual sequence override after issued documents can only set future next number and must never change historical issued document numbers.
pdf_immutability: Template edits create new template_version for future documents.
Re-render of an issued document must create a new explicit version/revision record and keep older version accessible.
PDF retrieval should allow version selection for audit purposes.
telegram_and_deepseek: Telegram authorization must use stable chat ID/user ID, not username only.
DeepSeek output must be parsed into strict JSON schema.
Customer name, address, item description, and notes can contain prompt injection; treat them as data only.
Server recomputes all totals and refuses mismatches.
Confirm issue requires a confirmation token bound to company_id, draft_id, draft_hash, total, and user/chat ID.
DeepSeek outage must degrade to manual draft entry, not block core UI.
company_data_security: Attachment paths must be private and validated.
Signed URLs need short TTL and authorization checks.
Renderer must not load arbitrary local file paths from user-controlled input.
Audit logs must record company_id, actor, IP, user agent, action, and before/after summary.
einvoice_metadata: Store supplier and buyer TIN/registration data at document issue time as snapshots.
Store line-level classification, tax code, tax rate, tax amount, and exemption reason.
Store currency and FX rate snapshot for non-MYR documents.
Do not submit to MyInvois in v1.
Before Phase 5, re-check official MyInvois SDK schema and code lists.
amount_in_words: Generate words from exact numeric grand_total after rounding rules are applied.
Never let amount-in-words disagree with numeric total.
If sen is 00, use template configured style: SAHAJA or DAN SIFAR SEN.
Negative amount words are allowed only for credit/debit/refund-style documents with explicit sign policy.
Currency wording must be configurable for non-MYR future support.
Generated words are snapshotted at issue time so future wording rule changes do not mutate issued documents.
Draft preview may regenerate, but issue snapshot freezes the text.
multipage_pdf: A4 item tables must support multiple pages.
Repeat item table header on every continuation page.
Keep each item row together where possible; long descriptions wrap cleanly.
Totals, amount-in-words, bank details, terms, and signature/chop appear only on final main document page.
Footer/signature/chop must never overlap item rows.
Page numbering is recommended as Page X of Y and may be template-controlled.
Long customer addresses and project descriptions wrap without pushing metadata into item table.
If the document is a DO and template says prices are hidden, continuation pages must preserve hidden-price layout.
Wehdah main invoice/quotation pages must come before artwork pages by default.
Artwork pages append after all main document pages.
number_preview: Number preview is a format example only and must never reserve or expose the actual next sequence as a promise.
issue_time_snapshots: Snapshot issuer, buyer, bank, terms, tax, currency/FX, template_version_id, issue timezone, numeric total, and amount_in_words_text at issue time.

schema_api_recommendations.status: Proposed audit recommendations only, not final migrations.

tables_to_add_or_refine.name: idempotency_keys
tables_to_add_or_refine.purpose: Prevent duplicate create/issue/send operations from retries.
tables_to_add_or_refine.api_impact: POST create/issue endpoints require or accept Idempotency-Key.

tables_to_add_or_refine.name: document_status_history
tables_to_add_or_refine.purpose: Fast workflow history separate from broader audit log.
tables_to_add_or_refine.api_impact: Document detail and audit screens can show status progression quickly.

tables_to_add_or_refine.name: payment_allocations
tables_to_add_or_refine.purpose: Support one payment across multiple invoices and partial payments.
tables_to_add_or_refine.api_impact: Receipt/payment endpoints can allocate one payment to one or more documents.

tables_to_add_or_refine.name: pdf_renders
tables_to_add_or_refine.purpose: Store immutable rendered PDFs by document and version.
tables_to_add_or_refine.api_impact: GET /documents/{id}/pdf?version=N retrieves immutable render by version.
tables_to_add_or_refine.dedup_guardrail: Any dedup must be company and document scoped; never serve a PDF across companies based only on content_hash.

tables_to_add_or_refine.name: document_lines e-Invoice fields
tables_to_add_or_refine.purpose: Avoid future rework by storing line-level MyInvois-related values.
constraints: validate by configured code list when e-Invoice metadata mode is enabled
tables_to_add_or_refine.api_impact: Line item API accepts optional tax/classification metadata.

tables_to_add_or_refine.name: documents currency snapshot fields
tables_to_add_or_refine.purpose: Support global use and future e-Invoice currency requirements.
constraints: check(currency = 'MYR' or fx_rate_snapshot is not null)
tables_to_add_or_refine.api_impact: Document create/update API must capture currency and FX rate for non-MYR documents.

tables_to_add_or_refine.name: numbering_policies
tables_to_add_or_refine.purpose: Freeze configurable document numbering per company and document type before issue-time sequence allocation.
constraints: unique active policy per company_id + document_type
tables_to_add_or_refine.api_impact: Admin configuration endpoint returns non-binding preview examples only.

tables_to_add_or_refine.name: documents amount-in-words fields
tables_to_add_or_refine.purpose: Control optional amount-in-words display and preserve issued wording snapshot.
proposed_columns: show_amount_in_words boolean not null default false
constraints: amount_in_words_text_snapshot required on issue when show_amount_in_words is true
tables_to_add_or_refine.api_impact: Document draft/update API can toggle display; issue API freezes generated wording.

tables_to_add_or_refine.name: documents issue snapshot fields
tables_to_add_or_refine.purpose: Freeze structured issued data independently from mutable company/customer profiles.
tables_to_add_or_refine.api_impact: Issued document API returns snapshots for issued views instead of live profile-derived values.

api_changes.rule: Server must compare draft_hash and recalculated total before allocating official number.
Return immutable rendered PDF version after authorization and company scope check.
Map Telegram chat/user ID to app user and allowed companies before any draft mutation.
Validate MIME, extension, size, company scope, and store original files privately.

must_include_risks: Mixed workbook sheet leaking into template seed
risk_register_requirements.cross_reference_requirement: Each mitigation should reference the revised plan or proposed schema/API section that explains the control.

Wehdah Solution: Canonical address spelling Unit vs Unite
Bank account holder names for Hong Leong Islamic and Bank Islam
Persada Gemilang Global: Canonical address wording between workbook and letterhead
Whether website appears on invoice/receipt PDFs

workflow_decisions: Whether Virtue Damsel is in v1 UI or profile-only placeholder.
Whether refund_note remains future metadata-only or becomes visible after v1.
Whether Persada letterhead is used for formal letters only or also for billing PDFs.
Whether thermal receipt is required for all companies or only receipt/cash bill flows.
Whether old document numbers should ever be stored as legacy references in notes, not sequences.
Final numbering pattern per company and document type, including Persada.
Whether amount-in-words defaults on or off per company and document type.
Whether .00 sen wording uses SAHAJA or DAN SIFAR SEN per template family.
Whether thermal receipts should ever show amount-in-words despite 60mm width constraints.
Maximum preferred item rows per PDF page for each template family before continuation page.

phase_ordering_requirements.accepted_change: Insert Phase 0.5 between audit closure and scaffold.

required_phases.name: Audit closure
required_phases.scope: Finalize Claude/ChatGPT audit findings and confirm no locked decision changed.
required_phases.gate: Audit output accepted and no unresolved blocker for scope freeze.

required_phases.name: Confirm missing info and v1 scope freeze
required_phases.scope: Confirm company identity gaps, numbering prefixes, Virtue Damsel v1 inclusion, official_receipt taxonomy, amount-in-words behavior, multi-page PDF behavior, deployment inputs, and PDF identity choices.
required_phases.gate: Missing-info checklist items tagged Phase 1/2 are resolved or explicitly deferred.

required_phases.name: Scaffold and infrastructure
required_phases.scope: Laravel/Inertia scaffold, Docker Compose, PostgreSQL, Redis, auth, baseline tests.
required_phases.gate: App boots locally and baseline tests pass.

required_phases.name: Company, customer, product, numbering foundations
required_phases.scope: CRUD, company scoping, sequence counters, audit log basics.
required_phases.gate: Per-company numbering and concurrency tests pass.

required_phases.name: Document workflows
required_phases.gate: Feature tests cover main lifecycle and immutable issue transition.

required_phases.name: PDF, artwork, and thermal templates
required_phases.scope: Wehdah, NAS, Persada template families; artwork page; 60mm thermal.
required_phases.gate: Visual/PDF checks pass and no cross-company brand leak.

required_phases.name: API, Telegram, DeepSeek
required_phases.scope: Sanctum API, OpenAPI, Telegram webhook, DeepSeek draft parser, confirmation flow.
required_phases.gate: AI/Telegram cannot issue without valid confirmation token and idempotency key.

required_phases.name: Production deployment
required_phases.scope: Tencent Ubuntu deployment, SSL, queues, backups, monitoring, smoke evidence.
required_phases.gate: Production smoke and backup restore verification pass.

phase_ordering_requirements.acceptance_gate_rule: Each phase block in the future Claude output should include scope, deliverables, tests/manual checks, dependencies, and exit criteria.

verification_requirements: Confirm this JSON contains every required top-level key and no accidental omitted section.
Confirm source pack paths referenced in this JSON exist locally.
Confirm detailed Excel/PDF/image design facts are present for NAS, Wehdah, Persada, Virtue partial reference, and A To Z generic sheets.
Confirm locked decisions appear unchanged and are not contradicted elsewhere.
Confirm the placeholder-marker scan is clean for unfinished-task markers, fixme markers, and angle-bracket placeholder markers.
When Claude audit output is later written, confirm input pack remains byte-identical unless user explicitly asks to regenerate it.
When Claude audit output is later written, confirm diff contains only the seven new audit output markdown files.
When a PR is later opened, confirm PR body links to INDEX.md and states audit output only, no app code.
Confirm amount_in_words_contract exists and includes Malay MYR example with TIGA spelling.
Confirm multipage_pdf_contract exists and requires repeated item headers and final-page totals only.
Confirm canonical_source_registry exists and classifies mixed sheets as non-canonical or deny-for-seeding.
Confirm ChatGPT deep research corrections appear in the DeepSeek implementation plan.

final_recommended_output_files.purpose: This self-contained JSON for ChatGPT Think Mode crosscheck.
Short instruction file telling reviewers to read the JSON first and preserve locked decisions.
Future revised plan by audit angle.
Future implementation phase ordering with measurable gates.
Phase-by-phase implementation contract for DeepSeek v4 Pro.
Short instruction file for using the DeepSeek implementation JSON.
Source evidence from ChatGPT Think Mode deep research report.

questions_for_chatgpt_think_mode: Does this crosscheck preserve all seven locked decisions without contradiction?
Are Claude's proposed markdown deliverables sufficient for the audit pass, or should any additional file be added?
Are the proposed schema/API additions appropriate as Proposed audit guidance rather than final migrations?
Are the Excel/PDF/image design facts detailed enough for a downstream UI/PDF implementation agent?
Does the plan correctly prevent cross-company template, data, and numbering leakage?
Does the Telegram and DeepSeek workflow sufficiently prevent prompt injection, unauthorized issue, and total tampering?
Does the e-Invoice metadata-ready approach capture enough line-level and document-level fields while avoiding full MyInvois v1 submission?
Are the deployment hardening requirements sufficient for a single Tencent Ubuntu server?
Which missing information blocks scaffolding immediately, and which can wait until template finalization?
Is there any area where the Claude plan accidentally turns audit output into implementation code?

chatgpt_deep_research_report_summary.verdict: Conditionally acceptable as audit-output handoff, not acceptable as implementation baseline until Phase 0.5 is tightened and completed.
critical_blockers_to_encode: Canonical template isolation needs machine-readable allowlist and deny-for-seeding classification.
Numbering format must be frozen per company and document type before scaffolding choices rely on it.
Receipt taxonomy must distinguish official_receipt from thermal quick-print output.
high_priority_corrections_to_encode: Issue-time snapshots for issuer, buyer, bank, terms, tax, currency/FX, template version, and timezone.
Number preview must be format example only, not reservation.
payment_allocations must be mandatory for partial and multi-invoice payments.
Telegram confirmation token must bind draft_hash, company_id, user_id, chat_id, expiry, and idempotency.
PDF rendering and attachments need render sandboxing, asset allowlists, MIME sniffing, SVG sanitation/rasterization, path normalization, and signed URL controls.
AI governance must define retention, redaction, malformed JSON rejection, timeout/fallback, and hard prohibition against AI choosing company/final number/issue.
medium_priority_corrections_to_encode: Workbook detected_* metadata is heuristic only and never canonical master data.
Wehdah artwork order should be template-controlled by default: main document first, artwork after.
Acceptance criteria must include measurable tests for concurrency, year rollover, immutability, signed URL expiry, AI rejection, and backup restore.
Persada billing identity must be frozen before pixel-level template work.
Thermal receipt needs line-width, Unicode, QR, and margin tests.

canonical_source_registry.purpose: Machine-readable allowlist so mixed workbook sheets cannot leak into company identity, template, or numbering seeds.
canonical_source_registry.global_rule: Only canonical_for_render entries may drive v1 template implementation. non_canonical_reference_only may inform structure. deny_for_seeding must never seed live company data, templates, or sequences.

canonical_for_render.notes: Use only sheets with Wehdah identity and real invoice/quotation layout.
Strongest output reference for Wehdah invoice plus artwork behavior.

non_canonical_reference_only.notes: A To Z generic structures inside Wehdah workbooks; usable only as generic document structure references.

deny_for_seeding: Any old document number, customer, item, total, payment, or historical row from the Excel files

canonical_for_render.notes: NAS Ceria Vertex42/Arial quote and invoice visual family.
Use only after sheet-by-sheet classification; workbook is mixed.

non_canonical_reference_only.notes: Costing, inventory, hidden, or Virtue partial sheets; not customer-facing NAS templates.

deny_for_seeding: Bare historical numbers such as 3118, 2291, 1292, 3893, 4316 as production sequence truth

canonical_for_render.notes: Only canonical Persada billing sheet in the workbook.
Use assets for Persada identity; letterhead is formal-doc background unless user confirms billing usage.

non_canonical_reference_only.notes: Mixed A To Z, Virtue, and Wehdah sheets; not Persada canonical.

deny_for_seeding: Persada workbook sheets other than INV 1 for Persada company identity
Historical invoice no 202601030025 as production sequence truth

non_canonical_reference_only.notes: Partial mixed references only; not enough for final v1 tenant/template rollout.

deny_for_seeding: Any final Virtue template, bank, tax, or numbering seed until user confirms active v1 inclusion and supplies missing assets

non_canonical_reference_only.notes: Reusable generic structure only. Not a real company unless user later confirms.

deny_for_seeding: Company profile seeding as an actual tenant

amount_in_words_contract.purpose: Optional display of total amount in words on invoice, quotation, official receipt, and other amount-bearing documents.

fields_and_config.documents.amount_in_words_text_snapshot: text generated and stored at issue time

default_malay_myr_example.words: RINGGIT MALAYSIA SATU JUTA DUA RATUS TIGA PULUH EMPAT RIBU LIMA RATUS ENAM PULUH TUJUH DAN LAPAN PULUH SEMBILAN SEN

rules: Generate words from exact numeric grand_total after rounding rules are applied.
Never let amount-in-words disagree with numeric total.
If sen is 00, use template configured style: SAHAJA or DAN SIFAR SEN.
Negative amount words are allowed only for credit/debit/refund-style documents with explicit sign policy.
Currency wording must be configurable for non-MYR future support.
Generated words are snapshotted at issue time so future wording rule changes do not mutate issued documents.
Draft preview may regenerate, but issue snapshot freezes the text.

template_placement.Wehdah Solution: Near existing RM: amount-in-words section before or near Grand Total.
template_placement.Nas Ceria Services: Near total quote/invoice footer, outside the item table.
template_placement.Persada Gemilang Global: Near grand total/payment section matching INV 1 layout.
template_placement.official_receipt: Near amount/received total where space permits.
template_placement.thermal_receipt: Optional and default hidden because 60mm width is constrained.

required_tests: numeric total equals generated words source
snapshot unchanged after template wording setting changes

multipage_pdf_contract.purpose: Ensure invoice, quotation, DO, receipt, and related PDFs remain professional when item lists or descriptions require additional pages.
rules: A4 item tables must support multiple pages.
Repeat item table header on every continuation page.
Keep each item row together where possible; long descriptions wrap cleanly.
Totals, amount-in-words, bank details, terms, and signature/chop appear only on final main document page.
Footer/signature/chop must never overlap item rows.
Page numbering is recommended as Page X of Y and may be template-controlled.
Long customer addresses and project descriptions wrap without pushing metadata into item table.
If the document is a DO and template says prices are hidden, continuation pages must preserve hidden-price layout.
Wehdah main invoice/quotation pages must come before artwork pages by default.
Artwork pages append after all main document pages.

template_specific_rules.Wehdah Solution: Repeat item header on continuation pages; keep dark blue header identity; amount-in-words and grand total only on final main page; artwork pages after main document.
template_specific_rules.Nas Ceria Services: Preserve compact Vertex42/Arial table proportions; remove spreadsheet junk; wrap long masjid/project text cleanly.
template_specific_rules.Persada Gemilang Global: Use Persada brand header and stamp/signature final block; respect INV 1 layout and final-page totals.
template_specific_rules.A To Z generic: Use only generic document structures for DO, PO, notes, official receipt, and payment voucher; do not seed company identity.

required_tests: long item description across several lines
multi-page invoice with amount-in-words on final page only
multi-page quotation with validity/payment terms retained
artwork pages append after all main pages
visual regression for Wehdah, NAS, and Persada
